title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, was charged with aggravated battery for allegedly stabbing his friend at a Miami Beach bachelor party.
The incident followed a dispute over women they met at bars on Ocean Drive, police reports indicate.
Both men sustained injuries, with Brian Gordon receiving a stab wound and Dickerson needing hospitalization.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, marking their third defeat in four games this season.
Ilay Feingold scored for New England, but St. Louis scored three unanswered goals, including a set-piece goal by Chris Durkin.
New England's Leo Campana exited the match with an injury after playing only 21 minutes as a substitute.

title: Don’t be fooled by this fake court notice scam in Mass., NH, authorities warn
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts and New Hampshire residents warned of fake court notices demanding immediate fine payments or court appearances.
Scam notices show errors like misspelled state names, generic judge names, and non-matching court contact details.
Authorities advise not to scan QR codes or provide personal info; contact courts directly via official channels.

title: The trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons begins this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover police officer, is set to begin a bench trial for assault with a dangerous weapon.
Fitzsimmons was shot by colleague Patrick Noonan during an alleged confrontation while officers served a restraining order.
Conflicting accounts exist about the shooting incident and past allegations, with Fitzsimmons denying she endangered her child.

title: She’s running Boston for patients ‘rebuilding their lives step by step’
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lina Sinisterra Mazariegos, 44, from Colombia, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
She uses marathons to process emotions and draw parallels between running and overcoming life's challenges.
Sinisterra Mazariegos dedicates her race to Spaulding patients and professionals supporting recovery and independence.

title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at House of Blues with opening act The Chats on March 20, 2026.
The setlist included new songs like 'Enough Is Enough' and classics such as 'Hate To Say I Told You So'.
Frontman Howlin' Pelle Almqvist energized the audience with his engaging stage presence and showmanship.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer in 2023, McHugh overcame treatment challenges to resume running marathons.
McHugh is inspired by her late mentor, Susan Hurley, and running for friends and personal triumph over cancer.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Senator Ed Markey and others raise privacy concerns about Meta's facial recognition in smart glasses.
Lawmakers question Meta's data handling and demand answers by April 6 about opt-out options for users.
The letter comes as protests are planned for March 28, fearing the technology could suppress public dissent.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council postponed voting on a $2.65 million proposal to replace firefighter gear containing PFAS chemicals.
Mayor Koch proposed PFAS-free gear funding after the harmful chemicals were found in current equipment.
Firefighters expressed frustration and safety concerns over the delay, emphasizing increased cancer risks from PFAS.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor Cathleen DeSimone proposes transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to reduce long-term costs.
The zoo requires $57 million in repairs and currently costs $900,000 annually from the city's budget.
Zoo officials assure continued focus on animal care, urging public input as transition discussions proceed.
